draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers value snappy, click-based interactions over animated transitions when managing time-sensitive client work
facade.label: Instant response
facade.acceptImplies: Users prefer immediate, no-animation UI feedback; they find fluid transitions distracting in high-velocity workflows like checking invoices or updating project status
facade.rejectImplies: Users expect smooth, animated transitions to feel in control; instant feedback feels jarring and suggests the app is unpolished or machine-like

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to interfaces that feel personal and lived-in rather than sterile
facade.label: Warm sidebar
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers approachable, human-feeling design; likely values personality in tools; may favor warm color palettes, soft typography, and less corporate aesthetics
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clean, professional distance; values clarity over coziness; may lean toward cool tones, minimal ornamentation, and clear hierarchy

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers value snappy, click-based interactions over animated transitions when managing time-sensitive client work
evidence.implication: Users prefer immediate, no-animation UI feedback; they find fluid transitions distracting in high-velocity workflows like checking invoices or updating project status

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to a friendly, approachable voice that feels like a helpful colleague rather than corporate software
facade.axisTargeted: voice and personality: friendly vs professional
facade.label: Casual and warm
facade.acceptImplies: Users prefer conversational, human-centered language; dashboard messages should feel supportive; error states should be encouraging rather than stern
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer professional distance and clear authority; voice should sound competent and formal; relationships are transactional, not social

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer approachable, non-corporate UI that feels less intimidating than traditional CRM tools
facade.axisTargeted: Warmth vs. Formality in visual geometry
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Design language should prioritize accessibility and friendliness over corporate severity; curves over sharp angles likely resonate
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer clean minimalism, sharper boundaries, or more 'professional' CRM aesthetic; roundness might feel too casual or childish

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need persistent project navigation (sidebar) paired with detailed client/project cards rather than a dense list view, to balance quick access with rich information display.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure + information density (sidebar vs tabs, cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent, always-visible navigation anchors and prefers chunked, spacious information blocks over continuous scrolling lists.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer tabbed navigation, list-based layouts, or different information grouping—suggests tighter density or horizontal navigation pattern.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need quick visual summaries (cards) rather than dense tabular lists to scan client status at a glance
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data presentation vs key metrics focus
facade.label: Dashboard cards vs detail rows
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers summary cards showing 1-2 key metrics per client (status, last contact, next deadline) — favors scannable, visual information design
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers detailed row-based tables with all fields visible at once — values completeness and structured density over visual hierarchy

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/panel vs. distributed across top navigation and floating elements—reveals information architecture preference for freelance workflows
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business tool that feels corporate or a supportive coach
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free environment vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals mood preference for daily work in a CRM
Whether user prefers data shown as numbers/charts vs. narrative summaries and status indicators—reveals how they want to digest client and project info at a glance
Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate transitions—reveals preference for quick-fire workflows vs. thoughtful pacing in task management

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/panel vs. distributed across top navigation and floating elements—reveals information architecture preference for freelance workflows
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business tool that feels corporate or a supportive coach
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free environment vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals mood preference for daily work in a CRM
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data shown as numbers/charts vs. narrative summaries and status indicators—reveals how they want to digest client and project info at a glance
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate transitions—reveals preference for quick-fire workflows vs. thoughtful pacing in task management
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer gesture-based interactions (drag, swipe) over menu-driven reordering for managing task priorities, finding the tactile control more satisfying than instant-click alternatives
facade.label: Drag to reorder
facade.acceptImplies: Freelancers value fluid, physical control over their workflow—they want to feel agency through motion, not menus. This could signal openness to gesture-rich interfaces for timeline adjustments, invoice sorting, or project sequencing.
facade.rejectImplies: Freelancers find dragging cumbersome or distracting in professional contexts. They prefer explicit, instant commands (buttons, dropdowns) that don't require sustained interaction. Gestures feel like friction, not delight.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/panel vs. distributed across top navigation and floating elements—reveals information architecture preference for freelance workflows
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business tool that feels corporate or a supportive coach
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free environment vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals mood preference for daily work in a CRM
Whether user prefers data shown as numbers/charts vs. narrative summaries and status indicators—reveals how they want to digest client and project info at a glance
Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate transitions—reveals preference for quick-fire workflows vs. thoughtful pacing in task management

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura
